title: The Foundry — Governed Object Schema (Source of Truth)
description: Every important object that flows through the Foundry. A complete object describes how something enters, lives, changes, and leaves: it carries an owner, a state, and a lifecycle. Documents are inputs, not truth — the truth is this structured record.

object_id.description: Stable, dumb reference key. Means nothing on its own.

object_type.description: What kind of thing this is (the noun).

world.description: Where it came from. Outside world (customers, suppliers) or internal world (the company).

box.description: The governed action family this object is routed into. See boxes.json.

stream.description: Which governance stream (flow) this object belongs to.

owner_team.description: Who owns this object — who is accountable when it is wrong. Ownership is not exclusive permission.

state.description: Operational state. active = flowing, pending = waiting, blocked = stuck (a bottleneck).

commitment.description: How hard this object is. proposal = someone said it; workflow = being processed; truth = committed to the system of record.

confidence.description: Triage confidence — how sure the Foundry is about what this object is and what it affects.

aging_days.description: How long this object has sat in its current state. Operational pressure.

approver_role.description: Who must approve before this can commit to truth. Null if no approval required.

source_input.description: How this entered the Foundry. Documents are inputs, not the truth.

lifecycle.description: Disposition. How the object leaves: archive, recycle, scrap, replacement linked.

evidence.description: Supporting documents / context. Evidence and audit, not master data.

downstream.description: object_ids this object affects if it changes — the downstream impact.

system_of_record_ref.description: Reference in the system of record (SAP) once committed to truth. Null until committed.

created_at.description: When this object entered the Foundry (ISO date).

updated_at.description: When this object last changed state (ISO date).
